title: Archaeology of the City: Urban Planning in Ancient Israel and Its Social Implications
author: Ze'ev Herzog
publisher: Tel Aviv University, Institute of Archaeology
allowedUseNotes: Copyrighted monograph. Summaries in project's own words only.
summary: Urban-form study covering, among much else, the early Iron Age 'enclosed settlement' plan (dwellings ringing an open center, e.g. Beersheba VII) used as the comparative model for the Ziklag placeholder layout.
confidenceNotes: Card seeded from team knowledge; page-level citations to be added during the citation verification pass (M1).
